A three-chapter near-future novella set in 2041 Singapore, where a
cognitive-offload implant called "Marginalia" lets the wearer rent out
a subsymbolic chunk of their working memory to strangers. Audra, a
court stenographer, discovers her rented-out working memory is being
used to cache testimony she is forbidden by law to remember.

Three chapters: (1) the rental-detection protocol fails, (2) Audra
retraces the path back to the renter, (3) she confronts the choice to
keep the memory or flush it before trial.
